utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi joyce , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm good . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm doing well . so , i know the nurse told you about dax . i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you when we get started , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so , joyce is a 50 year old female with a past medical history significant for copd , congestive heart failure who presents for follow-up to an abnormal lab finding . so , joyce , i , i got the results of your lab , uh , your labs . your hemoglobin was low . uh , so , i asked them to schedule a follow-up appointment with me . so , how are you feeling ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been feeling really tired lately . over the past couple of months , i've noticed that my energy has really gone down . i used to be really active , um , just trying to be as healthy as i can be , running, climbing . um , i at least try to do an hour or two a day . and over the past month , it's gone slowly downhill . i've just been so tired and exhausted and i have n't been able to really keep up with the way i , i was g- was going with my exercise .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , now , have you had ... have you noticed any blood in your stools at all ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , no , not really . not , not at this time .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and are your stools , like , dark or charry or black looking ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . not that i've noticed .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and are you spotting a lot with your periods ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i'm not .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , and do you have any other ... i know that you are endorsing this fatigue and you feel kind of dizzy and that type of thing . do you have any other symptoms like chest pain , shortness of breath , fever , chills , body aches , anything ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . any weight loss ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , i've noticed a little bit , but that's because i think i've been doing really well with my exercise . um , but nothing too significant .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , let me ask you a little bit about how's the , the copd doing . i , i know that , you know , you've stopped smoking several years ago , but , you know , you still have that em- those emphysema changes on your chest x-ray . so , how are you doing with that ? how's your breathing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's been okay . um , i have n't been smoking . i ... after i quit , i quit cold turkey and i have n't gone back since . so , that's been doing well . i think , uh , during the changes of season , it gets a little bit harder for me to breathe , but i think that's just because of my allergies .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and then from a congestive heart failure standpoint , are you watching your diet , you're watching your salt intake ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . i've been doing really well with that . i've been staying away from the french fries , and all the other salty foods that i love to eat , uh , and everything looks great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , so , french fries are one of my favorite foods , that and fried chicken , so-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i give you a lot of credit for staying away from french fries .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , okay . so , you ... so , no swelling in your legs or any- anything like that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , not that i've noticed .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and you feel like you have a good support system ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . my boyfriend is , is great and i have , um , my brother right down the road .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . good . i'm glad to hear that . uh , so , let's go ahead . i wan na just do a quick physical exam , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . all right . well , your vital signs here in the office look good , so , i'm , i'm really happy to see that with the abnormal hemoglobin that we saw . um , i'm gon na just listen to your heart and lungs and press on your belly a little bit and i'll let you know if i find anything , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . okay . all right . so , on physical exam , you know , i , i do n't appreciate anything cervical lymphadenopathy . your heart sounds really good , but i do hear a , a slight two out of six systolic heart , uh , murmur , um , systolic ejection murmur on your heart exam . your lungs sound clear . your abdomen ... you know , you did have a little tenderness to palpation in your right lower quadrant on your abdominal exam , uh , but i do n't appreciate any lower extremity edema . so , all that means it that we , you know , we hear that heart murmur , which we heard in the past , um , and your belly had some tenderness , so we'll have to talk about that a little bit , uh , going forward , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: let's look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the hemoglobin . so , here , looking at this , you know , your hemoglobin level is 8.2. somebody like you should have a hemoglobin o- of about 13 , 14 , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , we need to investigate why it's low . hey , dragon , show me the anemia labs . okay . so , looking here at your labs , uh , everything looks good from , from this standpoint . i think some of your anemia labs are still pending at this time that's part of the workup .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , let's go over a little bit about the assessment and plan for you . so , you know , your main problem , this abnormal lab , this low hemoglobin that we found , so , you're anemic and we nee- ... you know , i'm waiting for those anemia labs to come back to find out exactly , you know , what type of anemia you have . i'd like to go ahead and schedule you for an endoscopy a- and a colonoscopy just to make sure that you're not bleeding from your , inside your belly , okay ? um , and i'm ... i'll be in touch when those labs come back to see what further workup we need to do , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for your next problem , the copd , i think you're doing great . you know , i , i do n't think that you , uh , need any further referrals at this time for that . i would just continue staying away from smoking . it does n't sound to be like you need any inhalers at this time . uh , for your third problem , your congestive heart failure , you know , i wan na continue you on the toprol , continue you on the lisinopril and we'll continue you on your current diuretic dosing of 20 , lasix 20 milligrams a day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm gon na go ahead and order that referral to g- to gastroenterology . they're the ones who will do the endoscopy and the colonoscopy , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , order the referral to gastroenterology . so , i'll be in touch . i'm gon na stay in close contact with you over the next week or so and , uh , we'll get this all sorted out , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . take care , joyce .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 50-year-old female with a history of COPD and congestive heart failure.
Follow-up for abnormal lab results.
The patient's hemoglobin level was found to be low.
The doctor asks how the patient is feeling.
The patient reports fatigue and decreased energy over the past two months, impacting her ability to exercise.
The doctor asks if the patient has noticed blood in her stool.
The patient denies blood in her stool.
The doctor asks if the patient's stools are dark or black.
The patient denies dark or black stools.
The doctor asks if the patient is experiencing abnormal spotting between periods.
The patient denies abnormal bleeding between menses.
The doctor asks about other symptoms including chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, chills, or body aches.
The patient denies chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, chills, or malaise.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced weight loss.
The patient reports minor weight loss attributed to exercise.
COPD
The doctor asks how the patient's breathing is and how she is doing with her COPD.
The patient reports she has quit smoking and experiences shortness of breath during seasonal changes due to allergies.
Congestive heart failure
The doctor asks if the patient is watching her diet and salt intake.
The patient reports she is following a low-salt diet and avoiding salty foods.
The doctor asks if the patient has any swelling in her legs.
The patient denies leg swelling.
The doctor asks if the patient has a good support system.
The patient reports a good support system with her boyfriend and brother.
The doctor declares the intent to perform a physical exam.
The patient's vital signs are normal.
Physical exam findings: no cervical lymphadenopathy, 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, clear lungs, right lower quadrant abdominal tenderness, and no lower extremity edema.
The patient's hemoglobin level is 8.2.
Anemia labs are mostly normal, but some are still pending.
The doctor plans to schedule an endoscopy and colonoscopy to investigate the anemia.
The doctor advises continuing current COPD management without inhalers and continuing current CHF medications: Toprol, lisinopril, and Lasix 20 mg daily.
Lasix 20 mg daily.
The doctor will order a referral to gastroenterology for endoscopy and colonoscopy.

case_description: abnormal lab finding; COPD;congestive heart failure
